description: Per-domain cache TTL, rate limits, source tier, and Terms-of-Service notes. Loops 1 and 3 consult this before every fetch. A cache hit (now < expires_at) skips the fetch and consumes 0 search budget. TTLs are tuned to signal volatility: momentum/community short, papers/patents long.

arxiv.org.notes: Use the arXiv API where possible. Honor the 3s min delay the API requests.

ssrn.com.notes: Often gated; use abstract pages + search snippets.

nature.com.notes: Mostly paywalled. Use abstracts + Nature news; mark [paywalled] when only headline retrieved.

semanticscholar.org.notes: Prefer the Semantic Scholar Graph API for citation velocity + influential-citation counts.

crunchbase.com.notes: Most data behind login. Use only free-tier fields + search snippets.

wellfound.com.notes: Formerly AngelList Talent. Login-gated for full data.

theinformation.com.notes: Paywalled. Use headline + search snippets; mark [paywalled — headline only].

sec.gov.notes: EDGAR Form D for raises. Honor the SEC's 10-rps cap; User-Agent must include a contact email per SEC policy.

sbir.gov.notes: SBIR/STTR award search — non-dilutive grant signal.

github.com.notes: Prefer the GitHub REST/GraphQL search API for stars/forks/contributor growth. Unauthenticated rate limit is low — cache aggressively.

news.ycombinator.com.notes: Use the Algolia HN Search API.

reddit.com.notes: Use the public JSON API (.json suffix). Respect Reddit's 60 rpm.

pypi.org.notes: Download stats via pypistats for adoption velocity.

patents.uspto.gov.notes: USPTO PatentsView API is the structured source-of-truth.

paywall_fallback_chain: search the headline for a syndicated / open mirror
use headline + abstract only, flag [paywalled — headline only]; never fabricate body content
